title: Privacy Policy
intro: HealthSync is a user-controlled Apple Health export and sync utility. This policy explains how HealthSync handles HealthKit data, local exports, user-configured server uploads, and app settings.

sections.title: Scope
sections.body: This policy applies to the HealthSync iOS app and the privacy information provided for App Store review. HealthSync is a utility for exporting or syncing Apple Health data that you choose to make available to the app.

sections.title: Health Data Access
sections.body: HealthSync reads Apple Health data only after you grant permission through the iOS Health permission sheet. You can choose which Health categories to allow, and you can revoke access at any time in iOS Settings or the Health app.
items: HealthSync requests read access only for supported HealthKit data types used by the export and sync features.
HealthSync does not write fabricated or inferred health records into Apple Health.
HealthSync does not diagnose, treat, monitor, prevent, or provide medical advice. It is a data export and sync utility, not a medical device or healthcare service.

sections.title: Export Destinations
sections.body: HealthSync is designed around user-selected destinations. Depending on the features available in the app version you use, you may export data locally on your device or send data to a server endpoint that you configure.
items: Local export: when you export to a local file or Files/iCloud Drive location, the exported file is controlled by you and by Apple's Files/iCloud Drive services, not by HealthSync servers.

Server upload: when you configure a server endpoint, HealthSync sends selected Health data to that endpoint at your direction. The endpoint may be your own server, a self-hosted service, or another destination you control.
HealthSync does not provide a general hosted health-data storage account for users and does not operate a backend service that stores users' HealthKit records for the app.

sections.title: Information We Collect
sections.body: The developer does not collect HealthKit records through HealthSync as part of normal app use.
items: On-device settings: HealthSync may store your chosen endpoint URL, sync state, HealthKit anchor state, last sync time, and local upload history on your device so the app can operate.
Health data selected for export: Health data is read from HealthKit only to create the local export file or upload payload that you initiate or configure.
Support communications: if you contact us, we may receive the contact information and troubleshooting details you choose to provide.

sections.title: Information We Do Not Collect
items: We do not collect, sell, rent, or trade your HealthKit data.
We do not use HealthKit data for advertising, marketing, profiling, data mining, credit decisions, employment decisions, insurance decisions, or similar secondary purposes.
We do not use third-party advertising SDKs or cross-app tracking in HealthSync.
We do not collect your Apple ID password, Health app credentials, contacts, photos, calendars, reminders, payment card details, or advertising identifier.

sections.title: User-Configured Servers
sections.body: If you enter a server endpoint, you are choosing where HealthSync sends the selected data. The developer cannot control or inspect servers that you configure yourself.
items: Before entering an endpoint, make sure you trust the server operator and understand how that server stores, processes, and protects uploaded data.
HealthSync sends data over the network only to the endpoint you configure for the sync feature.
A server endpoint may keep logs or store uploaded data according to its own configuration. That destination is outside the developer-operated HealthSync app unless explicitly stated otherwise.

sections.title: Local Files and iCloud Drive
sections.body: When HealthSync exports a local file, that file is stored where you choose through iOS file sharing or Files. If you save a file to iCloud Drive, Apple processes that file under Apple's iCloud terms and privacy practices.
items: You are responsible for managing, sharing, moving, or deleting exported files.
Deleting HealthSync may not delete files that you previously exported to Files, iCloud Drive, or another location.
Exported files may contain sensitive personal health information, so share them carefully.

sections.title: How We Use Information
items: To request HealthKit permission and read the Health data types you authorize.
To create local export files or upload payloads that you initiate or configure.
To remember local app settings, endpoint configuration, sync state, and local history on your device.
To support background or incremental sync where you enable and configure it.
To respond to support requests that you send to us.

sections.title: Sharing
sections.body: HealthSync does not share HealthKit data with the developer, advertisers, data brokers, or unrelated third parties. Data is shared only with destinations you choose, such as a local file location or a server endpoint you configure.

sections.title: Retention and Deletion
items: HealthSync keeps app settings and local sync state on your device until you change them, reset the app state, delete the app, or erase the device.
Local export files remain wherever you saved them until you delete them.
For user-configured server uploads, retention depends on the destination server you choose.
You can revoke HealthKit access at any time in iOS Settings or the Health app.

sections.title: Security
sections.body: HealthSync relies on iOS app sandboxing, HealthKit permission controls, and the transport security of the endpoints you choose. No export or transmission method is risk-free. Use HTTPS endpoints you trust, protect exported files, and avoid sharing sensitive health data with untrusted services.

sections.title: Children
sections.body: HealthSync is not directed to children under 13, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children through HealthSync.

sections.title: Changes
sections.body: We may update this policy as HealthSync changes. The latest version will be posted on this page, and material changes will be reflected by updating the effective date.

sections.title: Contact
sections.body: If you have privacy questions about HealthSync, contact us at keith.dh@hotmail.com.

footer: This policy is intended to describe HealthSync's privacy practices clearly for users and App Store review.
